title: engine.active_service not updated upon "podman machine start"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

In `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` there is an `active_service` setting in the `egine` section, which affects (among other things) the socket all remote commands use to contact the engine.

One should expect it to reflect the currently running machine, at the very least, as trying to contact a stopped machine while another one is running is not exactly productive.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create two or more machines
```
$ podman machine init machine1
...
$ podman machine init machine2
...
```
2. Start machine2
```
$ podman machine start machine2
...
```
3. Try communicating to podman
```
$ podman ps
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:58111: connect: connection refused
```
4. Fix `containers.conf`
```
$ sed -i '' -e 's/active_service.*/active_service = "machine2"/' ~/.config/containers/containers.conf
```
5. Try again
```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Starting a machine does not update the currently active engine in `containers.conf`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Starting a machine should update the currently active engine in `containers.conf`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Tue Oct 18 19:55:57 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.18.5
Built:        Wed Sep  7 21:59:25 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

This seems to be the latest available brew version.

comments.body:
@baude @ashley-cui PTAL
I think this might be intentionally designed to function in this way.

Currently this is intended behavior, as connections are not exclusive to podman machine, but rather podman-remote as well. `podman system connection default my-machine-connection` should set your default connection correctly without needing to manually edit containers.conf.
